rulings.Case Title: AMANDA HUANG VS. JASPIR CHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 15, 2026, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT AMANDA YAN TING HUANG'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Cross-defendant Amanda Huang's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the cross-complaint filed by Desoto Ca Company and Yasir Khan is denied. None of the three claims in the cross-complaint are barred or invalid because of the plaintiff's' dismissal of claims against Huang. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIS PURIS DURAND VS. SOON YE LOUIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Court'S Order Compelling Plaintiff'S Further Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Order That Plaintiff And His Attorney Pay Monetary Sanction Of $500.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Gayle M. Kono; Request For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff And His Attorney Julio J. Ramos
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 15, 2026, line 2. DEFENDANT SOON YE LOUIE Motion To Enforce Court's Order Compelling Plaintiff's Further Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Order That Plaintiff And His Attorney Pay Monetary Sanction Of $500.00. Defendant Soon Ye Louie's motion to enforce court's order and for monetary sanctions is granted in part. Due to plaintiff Elvis Durand's failure to comply with the order filed August 28, 2025 and to offer any excuse for his non-compliance, an evidence sanction is imposed against him barring him from introducing any evidence at trial that was requested in any of the form interrogatories he was required to provide responses by the August 28, 2025 order. Because he has committed discovery abuse, Durand must also pay an additional $690 in monetary sanctions to Soon Ye Louie no later than February 15, 2026. No opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. SVES GO, LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One From The Gap, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 15, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT SVES GO, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One From The Gap, Inc. Defendant SVES GO, LLC's motion to compel further responses to its set one form interrogatories is granted in part and its request for monetary sanction is denied. Gap must serve verified code-compliant further responses to form 17.1 regarding RFAs 1-2, 5-6, and 9-10 without objections no later than January 29, 2026. The motion is not untimely. The fairer and better reading of Allender's July 10, 2025 4:09pm and August 4, 2025 7:23am emails is that Gap agreed to extend the deadline for the filing of this motion to 45 days after Gap's anticipated amended responses to SVES's first sets of form and special interrogatories and requests for admission. Although Gap never served amended responses to any of that discovery, it never disclaimed in writing that it would do so. Gap's lack of discovery relevance objection to form interrogatories 3.6 and 3.7 are sustained. Gap's responses to form interrogatories 4.1 and 4.2 are responsive and sufficient. Given the disparate allegations of wrongdoing in the complaint and cross-complaint, form interrogatories 12.1, 12.2 and 12.3 which are based on the word "incident" are not appropriate for this case. Gap's objections to form interrogatory 17.1 regarding RFAs 1-2, 5-6, and 9-10 lack merit and are overruled. Gap's responses to form interrogatory 17.1 regarding RFAs 3-4, 7-8 and 11-18 are responsive and sufficient. Neither side acted without substantial justification, thereby precluding any award of monetary sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. SVES GO, LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One From The Gap, Inc.; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 15, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT SVES GO, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One From The Gap, Inc.; Request For Sanctions. Defendant SVES GO, LLC's motion to compel further responses to its set one requests for admission is granted in part and its request for monetary sanction is denied. Gap must serve verified code-compliant further responses to RFAs 1-2, 5-6, and 9-10 without objections no later than January 29, 2026. The motion is not untimely. The fairer and better reading of Allender's July 10, 2025 4:09pm and August 4, 2025 7:23am emails is that Gap agreed to extend the deadline for the filing of this motion to 45 days after Gap's anticipated amended responses to SVES's first sets of form and special interrogatories and requests for admission. Although Gap never served amended responses to any of that discovery, it never disclaimed in writing that it would do so. Gap's objections to RFAs 1-2, 5-6, and 9-10 lack merit and are overruled. Gap's responses to RFAs 3 and 4 when read in conjunction with its response to form interrogatory 17.1 regarding those RFAs are responsive and sufficient. Gap's response to RFA 6 is evasive because it does not respond to the RFA in its most likely intended way of asking if Gap was aware that SVES was preparing to go public through an IPO at the time that SVES was making preparations to go public through an IPO. Gap's response to RFA 10 is non-responsive. Neither side acted without substantial justification, thereby precluding any award of monetary sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. SVES GO, LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One From The Gap, Inc.; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 15, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT SVES GO, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One From The Gap, Inc.; Request For Sanctions. Defendant SVES GO, LLC's motion to compel further responses to its set one special interrogatories is granted in part and its request for monetary sanction is denied. Gap must serve verified code-compliant further responses to special interrogatories 3 and 5 without objections no later than January 29, 2026. The motion is not untimely. The fairer and better reading of Allender's July 10, 2025 4:09pm and August 4, 2025 7:23am emails is that Gap agreed to extend the deadline for the filing of this motion to 45 days after Gap's anticipated amended responses to SVES's first sets of form and special interrogatories and requests for admission. Although Gap never served amended responses to any of that discovery, it never disclaimed in writing that it would do so. Gap's oppression and overbreadth objections to special interrogatories 1 and 4 are sustained. Gap's objections to special interrogatories 3 and 5 lack merit and are overruled. Neither side acted without substantial justification, thereby precluding any award of monetary sanctions. rulings.Case Title: MOUSA FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC VS. ABDEL HAMODA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER BY DEFENDANT (ABDEL HAMODA) TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ON OCTOBER 14, 2025
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 15, 2026, line 8. DEMURRER BY DEFENDANT TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ON OCTOBER 14, 2025. Defendant Abdel Hamoda's demurrer to the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Mousa Financial Services, LLC is overruled. Hamoda must file an answer to the second amended complaint no later than February 2, 2026. Hamoda's request for judicial notice is denied as to all exhibits except provisions of California codes and this court's register of actions. The face of the second amended complaint does not establish as a matter of law that plaintiff's sole claim is invalid or that plaintiff lacks the legal capacity to sue. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ELDON PEI VS. HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 15, 2026, line 5. PLAINTIFF ELDON PEI'S Motion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs And Expenses. Plaintiff Eldon Pei's motion for attorney's fees and costs is granted in large part. Defendant Hyundai Motor America shall pay reasonable fees of $20,140 plus reasonably incurred costs of $645.44 for a total of $20,785.44. The court credits the hours claimed by all of the timekeepers on behalf of plaintiff and believes that those hours are reasonable for the tasks identified in the billing records. Based on handling many lemon law cases, the court believes the hourly rates are a little high for attorneys and paralegals doing lemon law work in San Francisco and has reduced the hourly rates for the attorneys to a blended rate of $550 and for the paralegal to $250. The court finds that all claimed costs were reasonably incurred. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
